post.description: Get an aggregated summary of invocations: counts by status, counts by service, and duration percentiles (p50, p90, p99).

excludeCompleted.description: Exclude completed invocations (succeeded/failed/killed/cancelled) from the scan, returning only in-flight statuses.

post.summary: Get completed invocations breakdown over time
post.description: Time-bucketed breakdown of completed invocations (terminal outcomes only) into succeeded vs failed. Buckets `completed_at` into fixed-width [start, start + interval) buckets across the [startTime, endTime) window. `failed` currently aggregates failed + killed + cancelled.

startTime.description: Inclusive lower bound of the window (ISO 8601 date-time).

endTime.description: Exclusive upper bound of the window (ISO 8601 date-time).

interval.description: Bucket width as an ISO 8601 duration, e.g. PT5M, PT1H, P1D.

post.description: Get merged status and deployment ids for multiple invocations.

get.description: Get the error details for a paused invocation. Returns the error that caused the invocation to be paused, including the message, stack trace, error code, and related command information.
Get the most recent transient (retry) error for an invocation, read from sys_journal_events. Returns the error message, stack trace, error code, and related command information.

Get payload data for a specific journal entry. This endpoint returns the potentially large payload fields (parameters, value, failure, headers) that are excluded from the lite journal response. Use this for on-demand loading of payload data.

200.description: Payload data for the journal entry

parameters.name: includePayloads
parameters.description: When true, includes full payload data (parameters, values, headers) in journal entries. When false or omitted, returns lite journal entries without payloads for better performance.

Aggregated, server-wide throughput and capacity metrics: per-second rates and gauges summed across all partition-processor leaders (metrics_processor), HTTP-ingress nodes (metrics_node), and durable logs (metrics_log).

post.description: Kill multiple invocations in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.
Cancel multiple invocations in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.
Purge multiple completed invocations in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.
Purge journals for multiple completed invocations in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.
Restart multiple invocations as new in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.
Resume multiple paused or suspended invocations in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.
Pause multiple running invocations in batch. All operations execute in parallel and results are collected.

parameters.name: serdeName

parameters.name: deployment
parameters.description: Optional deployment id; if omitted, resolve the latest deployment for the service.

200.description: Wire-format bytes returned by the target deployment

MetricsResponse.description: Aggregated, server-wide throughput and capacity metrics. Each field is summed across all rows of its source table (one row per partition-processor leader, HTTP-ingress node, or durable log).

invocations_per_sec.description: PROCESSOR (metrics_processor.invocations): new invocations started per second — +1 per new invocation (the Command/Input entry) on the partition-processor leader, summed across leaders.

events_per_sec.description: PROCESSOR (metrics_processor.events): journal events written per second — every journal entry on the leader counts as one event.

actions_per_sec.description: PROCESSOR (metrics_processor.actions): user-facing billing/usage actions per second — one action per journal command, metered by payload (one action per started 64 KiB: action_units = ceil(len / 64 KiB), min 1, so 1 byte -> 1 action and 65 KiB -> 2).

invoker_mibps.description: PROCESSOR (metrics_processor.invoker_to_service_throughput): throughput from the invoker to the service deployments, MiB/s — bytes the invoker streams to SDK endpoints (new commands + replay).

slots_available.description: PROCESSOR (metrics_processor.invoker_available_slots): invoker concurrency slots free for new invocations, summed across partitions. A partition whose invoker is unlimited or not yet running reports slots as (0, 0).

slots_used.description: PROCESSOR (metrics_processor.invoker_used_slots): invoker concurrency slots in use (capacity - available), summed across partitions — how many invocations are concurrently executing.

ingress_mibps.description: INGRESS (metrics_node.throughput): ingress throughput, MiB/s — request + response body bytes seen at the HTTP ingress, turned into a rate by the sampler.

connections.description: INGRESS (metrics_node.current_connections): currently open ingress connections (live gauge).

waiting.description: INGRESS (metrics_node.waiting_invocations): admitted in-flight ingress requests blocked awaiting a result — request-response calls and attaches only; fire-and-forget sends are deliberately excluded.

log_mibps.description: DURABLE LOG (metrics_log.append_rate): bytes appended to Bifrost per second, MiB/s — summed across all logs, i.e. total durable-log write throughput.

deployment.description: Deployment selection strategy: 'keep' uses the current deployment, 'latest' uses the latest deployment
Deployment selection strategy: 'keep' uses the current deployment, 'latest' uses the latest deployment

error.description: The error message for this invocation

failedInvocationIds.description: List of invocation IDs that failed to process with their error messages

hasMore.description: Whether there are more pages available (only for filter-based requests)

lastCreatedAt.description: The created_at timestamp of the last invocation in the current page, used for pagination

invocation_ids.description: List of invocation IDs to operate on (max 1000)
List of invocation IDs to resume (max 1000)

deployment.description: Deployment ID to use when resuming (applies to all invocations)

invocation_ids.description: List of invocation IDs to restart (max 1000)

deployment.description: Deployment ID to use (applies to all invocations)

PatchDeploymentId.description: Specifies which deployment to use when resuming or restarting an invocation

succeeded.description: Invocation IDs that were successfully processed

failed.description: Invocations that failed with error details

succeeded.description: Successfully restarted invocations with their new IDs

failed.description: Invocations that failed with error details

JournalEntryPayloads.description: Payload data for a journal entry. Contains the potentially large fields that are excluded from the lite journal response.

parameters.description: Input parameters for Input, Call, and OneWayCall entries

value.description: Success result value for completed entries (Run, Call, GetState, Output, Awakeable, etc.)

failure.description: Failure details when the entry result is a failure

headers.description: HTTP headers for Input, Call, and OneWayCall entries

PausedErrorResponse.description: Error details for a paused invocation

message.description: The error message that caused the invocation to be paused

relatedCommandName.description: Name of the command that caused the error

relatedCommandType.description: Type of the command that caused the error

relatedCommandIndex.description: Index of the command that caused the error

pausedAt.description: Timestamp when the invocation was paused

TransientErrorResponse.description: Most recent transient (retry) error for an invocation

relatedCommandName.description: Name of the command that caused the error

relatedCommandType.description: Type of the command that caused the error

relatedCommandIndex.description: Index of the command that caused the error

failedAt.description: Timestamp when the transient error occurred

CompletedInvocationsBreakdownResponse.description: Time-bucketed breakdown of completed invocations (terminal outcomes only) into succeeded vs failed over a [startTime, endTime) window. NOTE: `failed` currently aggregates failed + killed + cancelled; these may be split into their own fields later.

startTime.description: Inclusive lower bound of the window, echoed from the request.

endTime.description: Exclusive upper bound of the window, echoed from the request.

interval.description: Bucket width as an ISO 8601 duration, echoed from the request.

buckets.description: Contiguous, zero-filled buckets ordered by start ascending. Each bucket covers [start, end).

start.description: Inclusive start timestamp of the bucket.

end.description: Exclusive end timestamp of the bucket (start + interval); equals the next bucket's start.

succeeded.description: Invocations that completed successfully (completion_result = success) within the bucket.

failed.description: Invocations that completed unsuccessfully within the bucket. Currently includes failed, killed, and cancelled.
